utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi , anna . how are you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm doing okay . how are you ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm doing okay . so i know the nurse told you about dax . i'd like to tell dax a little bit about you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: all right .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , anna is a 44-year-old female with a past medical history significant for arthritis , gout , and reflux , who presents today for follow up of her chronic problems .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , anna , it's been probably about six months since i've seen you . how are you doing ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm doing okay . um , my arthritis is starting to get better . um , i've been trying to move my body , doing pilates , lifting weights , um , and that's , kind of , helped me build up some muscle , so the joint pain is , has been going away .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . yeah . i know you were having , you know , some problems with your right knee , uh , and we sent you for physical therapy . so , so that's going well ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . the physical therapy's gone really well . i've built up my strength back and it's been really great .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so you feel like you're able to walk a little bit further now ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yup . i'm walking about a mile , a mile and a half a day .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . great . that's good . i'm glad to hear that . okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and then , in terms of your gout , um , how are you doing with that ? i know you had an episode of gout of your , your right first big toe , um , about two months ago . how are you doing with that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm doing , doing well . the medication helped it , you know , go down and go away . hopefully , , it does n't come back .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and are you taking the allopurinol that i prescribed ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and no issues with that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: nope .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . great . um , no further flare ups ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . great . all right .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and then , you know , how about your reflux ? we had placed you on , um , omeprazole , you know , to help with some of those symptoms and i know that you were gon na do some dietary modifications . how are you doing with that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so , i started to make some dietary modifications . unfortunately , i have n't cut the stone out quite yet . um , i've still been having some episodes and , and throwing up in the mornings , um , things like that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you're throwing up in the morning ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yup .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: like , just , like , reflux into your throat or are you actually vomiting ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , actually vomiting .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . that's a problem .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yup .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . well , um , let's talk about any other symptoms that you might have . have you had any abdominal pain ? um , diarrhea ? um , do you feel like your belly's bigger than usual ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , the , the first and the last . so , i've been having some abdominal pain and then i feel like i'm bloated all the time .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and when was your last bowel movement ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , probably two days ago .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . was it normal ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . any blood ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . and any weight loss ? anything like that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , not that i've noticed .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and any fever or chills ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . uh , well , sounds like we just did the review of systems with you . it sounds like you're endorsing this , you know , nausea , vomiting , abdominal distension . um , any other symptoms ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no ? okay . all right . well , i wan na go ahead and do a quick physical exam . okay ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , show me the vital signs . all right . well , your , your vital signs here look quite good . all right . so , i'm , i'm reassured by that . i'm just gon na check out your heart and lungs and your belly and , and l- let you know what i find , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . so , on physical examination , you know , everything looks good . your heart sounds good . your lungs sound good . you know , on your abdominal exam , you do have some pain to your right upper quadrant when i press on it , um , and there's no rebound or guarding and there's no peritoneal signs and your right knee does show a little bit of , uh , an effusion there and there's , uh , some slight pain to palpation and some decreased range of motion .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so what does that mean , you know ? that means that you have some findings on your belly exam that concern me about your gall bladder , okay ? so , we'll have to look into that and then , um , your right knee looks a little swollen , but you know , we know you have some arthritis there , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: let's take a look at some of your results . hey , dragon , show me the autoimmune panel . hey , dragon , show me the autoimmune labs .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so looking at your autoimmune panel here , you know , we sent that because , you know , you're young and you have , you know , arthritis and gout and that type of thing and everything seemed to be fine .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , show me the right knee x-ray .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , looking here at your right knee x-ray , you know , there's no fracture or anything , but you know , it does show that you do have that residual arthritis there , um , that we're , you know , we're working on improving so that we do n't have to do some type of surgery or intervention , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so let's talk a little bit about my assessment and plan for you , okay ? so , for your first problem , um , your reflux and your nausea and vomiting , uh , i wan na go ahead and get a right upper quadrant ultrasound to rule out any gallstones , okay ? um , and then i'm gon na check some labs on you . okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i want you to continue on the omeprazole , 40 milligrams , once a day and continue with those dietary modifications .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , for your second problem , your gout , um , you know , everything seems controlled right now . let's continue you on the allopurinol , 100 milligrams , once a day . um , do you need a refill of that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , i do actually .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , order allopurinol , 100 milligrams , once daily .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and then from your last problem , your arthritis , i'm very pleased with how your right knee is doing and i want you to continue pilates and using the knee and let me know if you have any issues and we can , and we can talk about further imaging or intervention at that time , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: any questions ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , no . that's it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . great . hey , dragon , finalize the note .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 44-year-old female with a history of arthritis, gout, and reflux presenting for follow-up.
Arthritis
The patient reports improvement in arthritis symptoms through pilates, weight lifting, and physical therapy.
The doctor asks if the patient's physical therapy for her right knee is going well.
The patient reports that physical therapy has been beneficial and helped restore her strength.
The doctor asks if the patient is able to walk further.
The patient is walking one to one and a half miles per day.
Gout
The patient had an episode of gout in her right first big toe two months ago.
The patient reports the gout episode resolved with medication.
The doctor asks if the patient is taking the prescribed allopurinol.
The doctor asks if the patient has any issues with the medication.
The doctor asks if the patient has had any further gout flare-ups.
Reflux
The patient reports ongoing morning vomiting despite dietary modifications.
The doctor asks if the patient is experiencing reflux or actual vomiting.
The patient confirms she is experiencing actual vomiting.
The doctor asks about abdominal pain, diarrhea, and bloating.
The patient reports abdominal pain and bloating.
The doctor asks when the patient's last bowel movement occurred.
The patient's last bowel movement was two days ago.
The doctor asks if the bowel movement was normal.
The patient confirms the bowel movement was normal.
The doctor asks if there was any blood in the stool.
The patient denies blood in her stool.
The doctor asks if the patient has experienced any weight loss.
The patient denies weight loss.
The doctor asks if the patient has had any fevers or chills.
The patient denies fevers or chills.
The patient's vital signs are normal.
Physical exam shows clear lungs, normal heart sounds, right upper quadrant abdominal pain without peritoneal signs, and right knee effusion with decreased range of motion.
The autoimmune panel results are within normal limits.
Right knee X-ray shows residual arthritis with no fractures.
The doctor will order a right upper quadrant ultrasound and additional labs to rule out biliary etiology.
Continue omeprazole 40 mg once daily.
The patient is instructed to continue dietary modifications.
Continue allopurinol 100 mg once daily.
The doctor asks if the patient needs a refill of allopurinol.
The patient confirms she needs a refill of allopurinol.
The doctor orders a refill for allopurinol 100 mg once daily.
The patient is instructed to continue pilates and knee usage, and to contact the doctor if issues arise.
